Log in to your Asana account at https://app.asana.com/.\n2. In the left sidebar, click on the workspace or organization name to ensure you are in the correct workspace.\n3. Click on the \u201cProjects\u201d tab in the sidebar. This will display a list of all projects in the workspace.\n4. By default, Asana shows all projects, but you can filter or sort them:\n   - Use the \u201cFilter\u201d option at the top to select \u201cActive\u201d projects (if available).\n   - Alternatively, look for projects that do not have the \u201cArchived\u201d label or are not in the \u201cArchived Projects\u201d section.\n5. Review the list of active projects displayed. Each project will show its name, team, and sometimes a brief description or status.\n6. opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/enhanced_oral_exam_20250805_100958.json:772:      "response": "To assign tasks to team members based on workload in Asana and optimize resource allocation, follow these comprehensive steps:\n\n1. Review Current Workload:\n   - Open your Asana workspace and navigate to the \u201cWorkload\u201d tab under the \u201cPortfolios\u201d feature (available in Asana Business and Enterprise plans).\n   - Here, you\u2019ll see a visual representation of each team member\u2019s assigned tasks and their workload over time.\n\n2. Assess Task Distribution:\n   - Identify team members who are overloaded (workload bar is red or above their capacity) and those who have available bandwidth (workload bar is green or below capacity).\n   - Click on individual team members to view their assigned tasks, due dates, and estimated effort (if effort is tracked via custom fields like \u201cEstimated Hours\u201d).\n\n3. Reallocate Tasks:\n   - For overloaded team members, select tasks that are less urgent or can be reassigned.\n   - Use the \u201cAssignee\u201d field to reassign these tasks to team members with available capacity.\n   - Adjust due dates if necessary to balance the schedule and avoid bottlenecks.\n\n4. Use Custom Fields for Better Allocation:\n   - If not already set up, create a custom field such as \u201cEstimated Hours\u201d or \u201cPriority\u201d for tasks.\n   - Enter estimates for each task to help visualize workload more accurately in the Workload view.\n\n5. Communicate Changes:\n   - Notify team members of any changes in their assignments using Asana\u2019s comment or notification features.\n   - Provide context for the reassignment to ensure clarity and maintain morale.\n\n6. Monitor and Adjust Regularly:\n   - Set a recurring reminder to review the Workload view weekly or bi-weekly.\n   - Adjust assignments as new tasks come in or as team members\u2019 availability changes.\n\n7. Automate Where Possible:\n   - Use Asana Rules to automate task assignments based on criteria such as project, priority, or workload.\n   - For example, set a rule to assign new tasks to the team member with the least number of tasks or lowest estimated hours.\n\n8. Reporting and Optimization:\n   - Use Asana\u2019s reporting features to track task completion rates, overdue tasks, and workload trends.\n   - Use these insights to further refine your resource allocation strategy.\n\nBy following these steps in Asana, you can ensure tasks are assigned based on real-time workload data, optimize resource allocation, and maintain team productivity and morale.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/enhanced_oral_exam_20250805_100958.json:830:      "response": "To set up Slack reminders for upcoming Asana task deadlines and ensure your team receives proactive deadline notifications, follow these comprehensive steps:\n\n1. Integrate Asana with Slack:\n   - Go to your Asana workspace.\n   - Click on your profile photo in the top right and select \u201cApps & Integrations.\u201d\n   - Search for \u201cSlack\u201d and click \u201cAdd to Slack.\u201d\n   - Authorize the integration by following the prompts and selecting the Slack workspace and channel(s) you want to connect.\n\n2. Configure Asana Project Notifications in Slack:\n   - In Asana, open the project you want to receive reminders for.\n   - Click on the \u201cCustomize\u201d button in the top right.\n   - Scroll to \u201cApps\u201d and select \u201c+ Add app.\u201d\n   - Choose \u201cSlack\u201d and select the Slack channel where you want notifications to appear.\n   - Choose which types of notifications you want (e.g., task creation, task completion, due date changes).\n\n3. Set Up Automated Deadline Reminders:\n   - In Slack, use the Asana app to set up reminders for upcoming deadlines.\n   - Type /asana link in your Slack channel to link a specific Asana project to that channel.\n   - Use the /asana create command to create tasks directly from Slack if needed.\n   - To receive reminders, ensure that \u201cDue date approaching\u201d notifications are enabled in the Asana-to-Slack integration settings.\n\n4. Customize Reminder Timing (Optional):\n   - Asana\u2019s Slack integration sends notifications when due dates are approaching, but for more granular control, use Slack\u2019s built-in reminders:\n     - In Slack, type /remind [@channel or @user] [what] [when].\n     - Example: /remind #project-team \u201cCheck Asana for tasks due tomorrow\u201d every weekday at 9am.\n   - Alternatively, use a third-party automation tool like Zapier:\n     - Create a Zap that triggers when a task in Asana is due soon (e.g., 1 day before).\n     - Set the action to send a custom Slack message to a channel or user.\n\n5. Test the Setup:\n   - Assign a test task in Asana with a due date in the near future.\n   - Confirm that the Slack channel receives a notification or reminder as configured.\n\n6. Communicate to Your Team:\n   - Inform your team about the new reminder system.\n   - Encourage them to check Slack for deadline notifications and to keep Asana tasks updated.\n\nBy following these steps, your team will receive timely Slack reminders for upcoming Asana task deadlines, ensuring everyone stays proactive and on track.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/enhanced_oral_exam_20250805_100958.json:892:      "response": "To create an automated workflow that syncs HubSpot contacts to Asana as tasks and notifies via Slack, follow these comprehensive steps using automation tools like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat). This workflow ensures that every new lead (contact) in HubSpot is instantly turned into an actionable task in Asana and the Operations team is notified in Slack, creating a seamless lead-to-task pipeline.\n\n**Step 1: Set Up Accounts and Permissions**\n- Ensure you have admin or integration permissions for HubSpot, Asana, and Slack.\n- Create or identify the Asana project where tasks should be created.\n- Identify the Slack channel or user(s) to receive notifications.\n\n**Step 2: Choose an Automation Platform**\n- Use Zapier or Make for no-code automation. Both support HubSpot, Asana, and Slack integrations.\n\n**Step 3: Build the Workflow (Example using Zapier)**\n\n1. **Trigger: New Contact in HubSpot**\n   - App: HubSpot\n   - Trigger Event: New Contact\n   - Connect your HubSpot account.\n   - Set up trigger filters if you only want certain contacts (e.g., leads with specific properties).\n\n2. **Action: Create Task in Asana**\n   - App: Asana\n   - Action Event: Create Task\n   - Connect your Asana account.\n   - Set up the task details:\n     - Project: Select the target Asana project.\n     - Task Name: Use HubSpot contact\u2019s name or company.\n     - Description: Include relevant contact details (email, phone, company, notes).\n     - Assignee: Assign to a specific team member or leave unassigned.\n     - Due Date: Optionally set based on lead urgency or a default value.\n     - Custom Fields: Map any additional HubSpot properties to Asana custom fields if needed.\n\n3. **Action: Send Notification in Slack**\n   - App: Slack\n   - Action Event: Send Channel Message (or Direct Message)\n   - Connect your Slack workspace.\n   - Set up the message:\n     - Channel: Select the appropriate channel (e.g., #new-leads).\n     - Message Text: Include key details, e.g.:\n       ```\n       :tada: New Lead Synced!\n       Name: {{HubSpot Contact Name}}\n       Email: {{HubSpot Contact Email}}\n       Asana Task: <{{Asana Task URL}}|View Task>\n       ```\n     - Optionally, tag relevant users or use Slack formatting for clarity.\n\n4. **(Optional) Add Filters or Paths**\n   - Use Zapier\u2019s filters to only continue the workflow for certain types of contacts (e.g., only MQLs).\n   - Add branching logic if different types of leads should go to different Asana projects or Slack channels.\n\n**Step 4: Test the Workflow**\n- Add a test contact in HubSpot.\n- Confirm a new task is created in Asana with correct details.\n- Confirm a Slack notification is sent with the correct information and link.\n\n**Step 5: Activate and Monitor**\n- Turn on the Zap (or scenario in Make).\n- Monitor the first few runs for errors or missed data.\n- Adjust mappings or filters as needed.\n\n**Step 6: Documentation and Training**\n- Document the workflow for your team, including:\n  - Trigger criteria (which contacts are synced)\n  - Asana project/task details\n  - Slack notification format and channel\n- Train the Operations team on how to use and troubleshoot the workflow.\n\n**Step 7: Maintenance**\n- Periodically review the workflow for changes in HubSpot, Asana, or Slack APIs.\n- Update field mappings if your lead or task structure changes.\n\n---\n\n**Summary of Tools Used:**\n- **HubSpot:** Source of new leads/contacts.\n- **Asana:** Destination for actionable tasks.\n- **Slack:** Real-time team notifications.\n- **Zapier/Make:** Automation platform to connect all apps.\n\n**Outcome:**  \nEvery new HubSpot contact is automatically turned into a detailed Asana task and the Operations team is instantly notified in Slack, ensuring no lead is missed and the pipeline is fully automated from lead capture to task assignment and team awareness.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/enhanced_oral_exam_20250805_100958.json:959:          "raw": "To create an automated workflow that syncs HubSpot contacts to Asana as tasks and notifies via Slack, follow these comprehensive steps using automation tools like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat). This workflow ensures that every new lead (contact) in HubSpot is instantly turned into an actionable task in Asana and the Operations team is notified in Slack, creating a seamless lead-to-task pipeline.\n\n**Step 1: Set Up Accounts and Permissions**\n- Ensure you have admin or integration permissions for HubSpot, Asana, and Slack.\n- Create or identify the Asana project where tasks should be created.\n- Identify the Slack channel or user(s) to receive notifications.\n\n**Step 2: Choose an Automation Platform**\n- Use Zapier or Make for no-code automation. Both support HubSpot, Asana, and Slack integrations.\n\n**Step 3: Build the Workflow (Example using Zapier)**\n\n1. **Trigger: New Contact in HubSpot**\n   - App: HubSpot\n   - Trigger Event: New Contact\n   - Connect your HubSpot account.\n   - Set up trigger filters if you only want certain contacts (e.g., leads with specific properties).\n\n2. **Action: Create Task in Asana**\n   - App: Asana\n   - Action Event: Create Task\n   - Connect your Asana account.\n   - Set up the task details:\n     - Project: Select the target Asana project.\n     - Task Name: Use HubSpot contact\u2019s name or company.\n     - Description: Include relevant contact details (email, phone, company, notes).\n     - Assignee: Assign to a specific team member or leave unassigned.\n     - Due Date: Optionally set based on lead urgency or a default value.\n     - Custom Fields: Map any additional HubSpot properties to Asana custom fields if needed.\n\n3. **Action: Send Notification in Slack**\n   - App: Slack\n   - Action Event: Send Channel Message (or Direct Message)\n   - Connect your Slack workspace.\n   - Set up the message:\n     - Channel: Select the appropriate channel (e.g., #new-leads).\n     - Message Text: Include key details, e.g.:\n       ```\n       :tada: New Lead Synced!\n       Name: {{HubSpot Contact Name}}\n       Email: {{HubSpot Contact Email}}\n       Asana Task: <{{Asana Task URL}}|View Task>\n       ```\n     - Optionally, tag relevant users or use Slack formatting for clarity.\n\n4. **(Optional) Add Filters or Paths**\n   - Use Zapier\u2019s filters to only continue the workflow for certain types of contacts (e.g., only MQLs).\n   - Add branching logic if different types of leads should go to different Asana projects or Slack channels.\n\n**Step 4: Test the Workflow**\n- Add a test contact in HubSpot.\n- Confirm a new task is created in Asana with correct details.\n- Confirm a Slack notification is sent with the correct information and link.\n\n**Step 5: Activate and Monitor**\n- Turn on the Zap (or scenario in Make).\n- Monitor the first few runs for errors or missed data.\n- Adjust mappings or filters as needed.\n\n**Step 6: Documentation and Training**\n- Document the workflow for your team, including:\n  - Trigger criteria (which contacts are synced)\n  - Asana project/task details\n  - Slack notification format and channel\n- Train the Operations team on how to use and troubleshoot the workflow.\n\n**Step 7: Maintenance**\n- Periodically review the workflow for changes in HubSpot, Asana, or Slack APIs.\n- Update field mappings if your lead or task structure changes.\n\n---\n\n**Summary of Tools Used:**\n- **HubSpot:** Source of new leads/contacts.\n- **Asana:** Destination for actionable tasks.\n- **Slack:** Real-time team notifications.\n- **Zapier/Make:** Automation platform to connect all apps.\n\n**Outcome:**  \nEvery new HubSpot contact is automatically turned into a detailed Asana task and the Operations team is instantly notified in Slack, ensuring no lead is missed and the pipeline is fully automated from lead capture to task assignment and team awareness.",

Log in to your Asana account at https://app.asana.com/.\n2. In the left sidebar, click on the name of your workspace or organization to ensure you are in the correct workspace.\n3. Click on the \u201cProjects\u201d tab or section in the sidebar. This will display a list of all projects in the workspace.\n4. To filter for active projects:\n   - If you use Asana\u2019s default views, active projects are typically those not archived. Archived projects are hidden by default.\n   - If you see archived projects, you can filter them out by clicking the filter icon (three horizontal lines or funnel icon) and selecting \u201cActive\u201d or deselecting \u201cArchived.\u201d\n5. Review the list of active projects displayed. Each project will show its name, and you can click into each for more details.\n\nAlternatively, if you have access to Asana\u2019s advanced search:\n1. Click the search bar at the top and select \u201cAdvanced Search.\u201d\n2. Set the filter to \u201cProjects\u201d and add criteria such as \u201cNot Archived.\u201d\n3. Run the search to display all active projects.\n\nIf you are using the Asana API or integrations:\n- Use the endpoint GET /projects with the parameter archived=false to retrieve all active projects programmatically.\n\nHere is an example of what the list might look like (replace with your actual project names):\n\n- Marketing Campaign Q2\n- Product Launch Roadmap\n- Website Redesign\n- Customer Support Improvements\n- Weekly Standup Planning\n- Sales Pipeline Management\n\nThis list will give you a comprehensive overview of all active projects in your Asana workspace for your weekly standup review. Navigate to the main project workspace in your project management tool (e.g., Jira, Asana, Trello, ClickUp, etc.).\n2. Locate the option to create a new task, issue, or card (usually a \"+ New Task\", \"Create Issue\", or similar button).\n3. Click the button to open the new task creation form.\n4. Fill in the task details as follows:\n   - Title: Track New Feature Request\n   - Description: This task is created to track the progress and implementation of the newly requested feature. Please include all relevant details, requirements, and acceptance criteria for the feature. Assign appropriate team members and set due dates as necessary.\n   - Priority: Set according to the urgency of the feature request (e.g., Medium or High).\n   - Assignee: Assign to the relevant team member or product owner.\n   - Labels/Tags: Add labels such as \"feature request\", \"new feature\", or any project-specific tags.\n   - Attachments: If there are any supporting documents or specifications, attach them to the task.\n5. Review the information for accuracy and completeness.\n6. Click \"Create\", \"Add Task\", or the equivalent button to save the new task in the main project workspace.\n7. Notify relevant stakeholders (via comments, mentions, or notifications) that the new feature request task has been created and is ready for tracking.\n\nThis process ensures the new feature request is properly documented, tracked, and visible to all relevant team members in the main project workspace.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:97:        "agent_response_raw": "To create a task in Asana based on a new HubSpot deal and ensure synchronized workflows for the sales and project teams, follow these comprehensive steps:\n\n1. Integrate HubSpot and Asana:\n   - Use a native integration (if available) or a third-party automation tool like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat).\n   - In Zapier, create a new Zap with the following setup:\n     - Trigger: \"New Deal in HubSpot\" (choose the appropriate pipeline/stage if needed).\n     - Action: \"Create Task in Asana\".\n\n2. Configure the Trigger (HubSpot):\n   - Connect your HubSpot account to Zapier.\n   - Set the trigger event to \"New Deal\".\n   - Optionally, filter by pipeline or deal stage to only trigger for relevant deals.\n\n3. Set Up the Action (Asana):\n   - Connect your Asana account to Zapier.\n   - Choose the workspace and project where the task should be created.\n   - Map HubSpot deal fields to Asana task fields:\n     - Task Name: Use the deal name (e.g., \"New Deal: {{Deal Name}}\").\n     - Description: Include relevant deal details such as deal amount, close date, deal owner, company name, and any custom fields.\n     - Assignee: Assign to the relevant project manager or team member.\n     - Due Date: Set based on the deal close date or a standard turnaround time.\n     - Attachments/Links: Add a link to the HubSpot deal record for easy reference.\n\n4. Test the Integration:\n   - Create a test deal in HubSpot to ensure a corresponding task is created in Asana with all mapped details.\n\n5. Notify Teams:\n   - Optionally, add an additional action in Zapier to send a notification (e.g., Slack message or email) to alert the project team of the new task.\n\n6. Maintain and Optimize:\n   - Periodically review the integration to ensure it meets evolving workflow needs.\n   - Update field mappings or add custom logic as required.\n\nExample Asana Task Created:\n- Task Name: \"New Deal: Acme Corp Website Redesign\"\n- Description:\n  ```\n  A new deal has been closed in HubSpot.\n\n  Deal Details:\n  - Deal Name: Acme Corp Website Redesign\n  - Amount: $25,000\n  - Close Date: 2024-07-01\n  - Deal Owner: Jane Smith\n  - Company: Acme Corp\n  - HubSpot Deal Link: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/123456\n\n  Please review and initiate the project onboarding process.\n  ```\n- Assignee: John Doe (Project Manager)\n- Due Date: 2024-07-03\n\nBy following these steps, you ensure that every new HubSpot deal automatically creates a detailed, actionable task in Asana, keeping sales and project teams aligned and workflows synchronized.",

Integrate HubSpot and Asana:\n   - Use a native integration (if available) or a third-party automation tool like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat).\n   - In Zapier, create a new Zap with the following setup:\n     - Trigger: \"New Deal in HubSpot\" (choose the appropriate pipeline/stage if needed).\n     - Action: \"Create Task in Asana\".\n\n2. Configure the Trigger (HubSpot):\n   - Connect your HubSpot account to Zapier.\n   - Set the trigger event to \"New Deal\".\n   - Optionally, filter by pipeline or deal stage to only trigger for relevant deals.\n\n3. Set Up the Action (Asana):\n   - Connect your Asana account to Zapier.\n   - Choose the workspace and project where the task should be created.\n   - Map HubSpot deal fields to Asana task fields:\n     - Task Name: Use the deal name (e.g., \"New Deal: {{Deal Name}}\").\n     - Description: Include relevant deal details such as deal amount, close date, deal owner, company name, and any custom fields.\n     - Assignee: Assign to the relevant project manager or team member.\n     - Due Date: Set based on the deal close date or a standard turnaround time.\n     - Attachments/Links: Add a link to the HubSpot deal record for easy reference.\n\n4. Test the Integration:\n   - Create a test deal in HubSpot to ensure a corresponding task is created in Asana with all mapped details.\n\n5. Notify Teams:\n   - Optionally, add an additional action in Zapier to send a notification (e.g., Slack message or email) to alert the project team of the new task.\n\n6. Maintain and Optimize:\n   - Periodically review the integration to ensure it meets evolving workflow needs.\n   - Update field mappings or add custom logic as required.\n\nExample Asana Task Created:\n- Task Name: \"New Deal: Acme Corp Website Redesign\"\n- Description:\n  ```\n  A new deal has been closed in HubSpot.\n\n  Deal Details:\n  - Deal Name: Acme Corp Website Redesign\n  - Amount: $25,000\n  - Close Date: 2024-07-01\n  - Deal Owner: Jane Smith\n  - Company: Acme Corp\n  - HubSpot Deal Link: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/123456\n\n  Please review and initiate the project onboarding process.\n  ```\n- Assignee: John Doe (Project Manager)\n- Due Date: 2024-07-03\n\nBy following these steps, you ensure that every new HubSpot deal automatically creates a detailed, actionable task in Asana, keeping sales and project teams aligned and workflows synchronized.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:121:        "agent_response_raw": "To provide a detailed status of a specific Asana project, including tasks and progress, follow these steps:\n\n1. Access the Asana Project:\n   - Log in to your Asana account.\n   - Navigate to the specific project you want to review from the sidebar or search bar.\n\n2. Project Overview:\n   - At the top of the project, you\u2019ll see the project name, owner, and due date (if set).\n   - The \u201cOverview\u201d tab provides a summary, including project description, key milestones, and recent activity.\n\n3. Task List and Progress:\n   - Click on the \u201cList\u201d or \u201cBoard\u201d view to see all tasks.\n   - Each task displays its name, assignee, due date, and completion status.\n   - Sections or columns may represent phases or categories.\n   - Completed tasks are usually checked off or moved to a \u201cDone\u201d section.\n\n4. Progress Tab:\n   - The \u201cProgress\u201d tab (if enabled) shows a visual progress chart (e.g., % complete, number of tasks done vs. total).\n   - You can see recent status updates, blockers, and next steps.\n   - Project owners can post regular status updates with color-coded health indicators (On Track, At Risk, Off Track).\n\n5. Task Details:\n   - Click on any task to view details: description, subtasks, attachments, comments, and custom fields (e.g., priority, effort).\n   - Subtasks have their own completion status.\n\n6. Reporting and Metrics:\n   - Use the \u201cDashboard\u201d or \u201cReporting\u201d features to generate charts (e.g., tasks by status, overdue tasks, workload by assignee).\n   - Export data to CSV for further analysis if needed.\n\n7. Progress Tab:\n   - The \u201cProgress\u201d tab (if enabled) shows a visual progress chart (e.g., % complete, number of tasks done vs. total).\n   - You can see recent status updates, blockers, and next steps.\n   - Project owners can post regular status updates with color-coded health indicators (On Track, At Risk, Off Track).\n\n5. Task Details:\n   - Click on any task to view details: description, subtasks, attachments, comments, and custom fields (e.g., priority, effort).\n   - Subtasks have their own completion status.\n\n6. opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:145:        "agent_response_raw": "To assign tasks to team members based on workload in Asana and optimize resource allocation, follow these comprehensive steps:\n\n1. Review Current Workload:\n   - Open Asana and navigate to the \u201cWorkload\u201d tab under the \u201cPortfolios\u201d feature (available in Asana Business and Enterprise plans).\n   - Review each team member\u2019s current tasks, deadlines, and estimated effort (hours or points). This visual overview helps identify who is overloaded or underutilized.\n\n2. Gather Task Details:\n   - Ensure all tasks have clear due dates, priorities, and estimated effort assigned.\n   - If effort estimates are missing, collaborate with task owners to add them for accurate workload calculation.\n\n3. Analyze Resource Allocation:\n   - In the Workload view, look for team members whose workload bars are red (over capacity) or green (under capacity).\n   - Click on individual team members to see detailed breakdowns of their assigned tasks.\n\n4. Reassign Tasks for Balance:\n   - For overloaded team members, identify tasks that can be reassigned.\n   - Drag and drop tasks from overloaded members to those with available capacity directly in the Workload view.\n   - Alternatively, open the task, click \u201cAssignee,\u201d and select a new team member.\n\n5. Communicate Changes:\n   - Notify team members of any changes in their assignments via Asana comments or team messages.\n   - Provide context for reassignments to ensure clarity and maintain morale.\n\n6. Automate and Monitor:\n   - Set up Asana rules to automatically assign new tasks based on workload or role.\n   - Regularly review the Workload tab to monitor ongoing balance and make adjustments as needed.\n\n7. Continuous Improvement:\n   - Hold regular resource allocation meetings to discuss workload and gather feedback.\n   - Adjust estimation practices and task distribution based on team input and project outcomes.\n\nBy following these steps and leveraging Asana\u2019s Workload and automation features, you can ensure tasks are assigned efficiently, team members are neither overburdened nor underutilized, and resource allocation is continuously optimized.",

This visual overview helps identify who is overloaded or underutilized.\n\n2. Gather Task Details:\n   - Ensure all tasks have clear due dates, priorities, and estimated effort assigned.\n   - If effort estimates are missing, collaborate with task owners to add them for accurate workload calculation.\n\n3. Analyze Resource Allocation:\n   - In the Workload view, look for team members whose workload bars are red (over capacity) or green (under capacity).\n   - Click on individual team members to see detailed breakdowns of their assigned tasks.\n\n4. Reassign Tasks for Balance:\n   - For overloaded team members, identify tasks that can be reassigned.\n   - Drag and drop tasks from overloaded members to those with available capacity directly in the Workload view.\n   - Alternatively, open the task, click \u201cAssignee,\u201d and select a new team member.\n\n5. opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:153:        "agent_response_raw": "To set up Slack reminders for upcoming Asana task deadlines and ensure your team receives proactive deadline notifications, follow these comprehensive steps using the official Asana and Slack integration:\n\n1. **Connect Asana to Slack:**\n   - In Slack, go to the Apps section and search for \u201cAsana.\u201d\n   - Click \u201cAdd to Slack\u201d and follow the prompts to connect your Asana account.\n   - Authorize the integration by logging into your Asana account when prompted.\n\n2. **Choose the Slack Channel for Notifications:**\n   - Decide which Slack channel(s) should receive Asana task deadline reminders (e.g., #project-deadlines).\n   - In Slack, type `/asana link` and select the relevant Asana project(s) you want to monitor.\n\n3. **Configure Asana Notifications in Slack:**\n   - In the linked channel, type `/asana` to see available commands.\n   - Use `/asana settings` to customize which notifications are sent to the channel.\n   - Enable notifications for \u201cTask due soon\u201d and \u201cTask due date changed\u201d to ensure reminders are sent as deadlines approach.\n\n4. **Set Up Personal Reminders (Optional):**\n   - Team members can type `/asana my tasks` in Slack to see their upcoming tasks.\n   - They can also set up personal reminders in Asana by clicking the bell icon on a task and choosing \u201cRemind me\u201d (e.g., 1 day before due).\n\n5. **Automate Custom Reminders with Asana Rules (Advanced):**\n   - In Asana, open your project and click \u201cCustomize\u201d in the top right.\n   - Under \u201cRules,\u201d click \u201cAdd Rule.\u201d\n   - Choose a trigger like \u201cDue date is approaching\u201d (e.g., 1 day before due).\n   - Set the action to \u201cSend a message to Slack\u201d and select the appropriate channel.\n   - Save the rule. Now, whenever a task\u2019s due date is approaching, a reminder will be automatically posted in Slack.\n\n6. **Test the Integration:**\n   - Create a test task in Asana with a due date within the next day.\n   - Confirm that a reminder appears in the designated Slack channel at the expected time.\n\n7. **Best Practices:**\n   - Regularly review which projects are linked to which Slack channels to avoid notification overload.\n   - Encourage team members to use Asana\u2019s \u201cMy Tasks\u201d and Slack\u2019s personal reminders for individual accountability.\n   - Adjust notification settings as needed to match your team\u2019s workflow.\n\nBy following these steps, your team will receive timely Slack reminders for upcoming Asana task deadlines, ensuring everyone stays on track and proactive about meeting project goals.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:169:        "agent_response_raw": "To create an automated workflow that syncs HubSpot contacts to Asana as tasks and notifies via Slack, you can use integration tools like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat), which allow you to connect these platforms without custom code. Here is a comprehensive step-by-step solution using Zapier, which is widely used and user-friendly:\n\n**Step 1: Set Up Your Tools**\n- Ensure you have admin access to HubSpot, Asana, and Slack.\n- Create accounts on Zapier (https://zapier.com/) if you don\u2019t have one.\n\n**Step 2: Create a New Zap in Zapier**\n1. **Trigger: HubSpot New Contact**\n   - App: HubSpot\n   - Trigger Event: New Contact\n   - Connect your HubSpot account.\n   - Set up the trigger to fire when a new contact is created (you can filter by specific properties if needed).\n\n2. **Action: Create Task in Asana**\n   - App: Asana\n   - Action Event: Create Task\n   - Connect your Asana account.\n   - Set up the action:\n     - Choose the Workspace and Project where tasks should be created.\n     - Map HubSpot contact fields (e.g., Name, Email, Company, Phone) into the Asana task fields (e.g., Task Name, Description, Custom Fields).\n     - Optionally assign the task to a specific team member or set due dates based on your process.\n\n3. **Action: Send Notification in Slack**\n   - App: Slack\n   - Action Event: Send Channel Message (or Direct Message)\n   - Connect your Slack workspace.\n   - Set up the message:\n     - Choose the channel (e.g., #operations-leads) or user to notify.\n     - Customize the message to include key contact details from HubSpot and a link to the new Asana task (Zapier can return the Asana task URL).\n     - Example message:  \n       ```\n       :bell: New Lead Synced!\n       Name: {{HubSpot Contact Name}}\n       Email: {{HubSpot Contact Email}}\n       Asana Task: {{Asana Task URL}}\n       ```\n   - Optionally, use Slack\u2019s formatting for better readability.\n\n**Step 3: Test the Workflow**\n- Use Zapier\u2019s built-in test features to ensure that when a new contact is added in HubSpot, a corresponding task is created in Asana and a Slack notification is sent.\n- Adjust field mappings and message formatting as needed.\n\n**Step 4: Turn On the Zap**\n- Once testing is successful, turn on the Zap to make the workflow live.\n\n**Step 5: Monitor and Maintain**\n- Periodically check Zapier\u2019s task history for errors.\n- Update field mappings if your HubSpot or Asana structures change.\n\n**Optional Enhancements:**\n- Add filters in Zapier to only sync contacts that meet certain criteria (e.g., lead status, lifecycle stage).\n- Use Asana custom fields to track lead status or priority.\n- Add additional Slack actions, such as mentioning specific users or using Slack threads for follow-up.\n\n**Summary of Workflow:**\n1. New contact is created in HubSpot.\n2. Zapier automatically creates a new task in Asana with the contact\u2019s details.\n3. Zapier sends a notification to a designated Slack channel or user with the lead info and Asana task link.\n4. Operations team is instantly notified and can take action, ensuring a seamless lead-to-task pipeline.\n\n**Tools Used:**\n- HubSpot (CRM)\n- Asana (Task Management)\n- Slack (Team Communication)\n- Zapier (Automation Platform)\n\nThis setup ensures a fully automated, reliable, and scalable lead-to-task pipeline for your operations team, with real-time notifications and no manual intervention required.",

Log in to your Asana account at https://app.asana.com/.\n2. In the left sidebar, click on the name of your workspace or organization to ensure you are in the correct workspace.\n3. Click on the \u201cProjects\u201d tab or section in the sidebar. This will display a list of all projects in the workspace.\n4. To filter for active projects:\n   - If you use Asana\u2019s default views, active projects are typically those not archived. Archived projects are hidden by default.\n   - If you see archived projects, you can filter them out by clicking the filter icon (three horizontal lines or funnel icon) and selecting \u201cActive\u201d or deselecting \u201cArchived.\u201d\n5. Review the list of active projects displayed. Each project will show its name, and you can click into each for more details.\n\nAlternatively, if you have access to Asana\u2019s advanced search:\n1. Click the search bar at the top and select \u201cAdvanced Search.\u201d\n2. Set the filter to \u201cProjects\u201d and add criteria such as \u201cNot Archived.\u201d\n3. Run the search to display all active projects.\n\nIf you are using the Asana API or integrations:\n- Use the endpoint GET /projects with the parameter archived=false to retrieve all active projects programmatically.\n\nHere is an example of what the list might look like (replace with your actual project names):\n\n- Marketing Campaign Q2\n- Product Launch Roadmap\n- Website Redesign\n- Customer Support Improvements\n- Weekly Standup Planning\n- Sales Pipeline Management\n\nThis list will give you a comprehensive overview of all active projects in your Asana workspace for your weekly standup review. opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:289:          "raw": "To list all active projects in your Asana workspace, follow these steps:\n\n1. Log in to your Asana account at https://app.asana.com/.\n2. In the left sidebar, click on the name of your workspace or organization to ensure you are in the correct workspace.\n3. Click on the \u201cProjects\u201d tab or section in the sidebar. This will display a list of all projects in the workspace.\n4. To filter for active projects:\n   - If you use Asana\u2019s default views, active projects are typically those not archived. Archived projects are hidden by default.\n   - If you see archived projects, you can filter them out by clicking the filter icon (three horizontal lines or funnel icon) and selecting \u201cActive\u201d or deselecting \u201cArchived.\u201d\n5. Review the list of active projects displayed. Each project will show its name, and you can click into each for more details.\n\nAlternatively, if you have access to Asana\u2019s advanced search:\n1. Click the search bar at the top and select \u201cAdvanced Search.\u201d\n2. Set the filter to \u201cProjects\u201d and add criteria such as \u201cNot Archived.\u201d\n3. Run the search to display all active projects.\n\nIf you are using the Asana API or integrations:\n- Use the endpoint GET /projects with the parameter archived=false to retrieve all active projects programmatically.\n\nHere is an example of what the list might look like (replace with your actual project names):\n\n- Marketing Campaign Q2\n- Product Launch Roadmap\n- Website Redesign\n- Customer Support Improvements\n- Weekly Standup Planning\n- Sales Pipeline Management\n\nThis list will give you a comprehensive overview of all active projects in your Asana workspace for your weekly standup review. If you need the actual exported list, you can select all projects and export them as a CSV from the Asana interface for sharing with your team.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:388:      "response": "To create a new task in the main project workspace for tracking the new feature request, follow these steps:\n\n1. Navigate to the main project workspace in your project management tool (e.g., Jira, Asana, Trello, ClickUp, etc.).\n2. Locate the option to create a new task, issue, or card (usually a \"+ New Task\", \"Create Issue\", or similar button).\n3. Click the button to open the new task creation form.\n4. Fill in the task details as follows:\n   - Title: Track New Feature Request\n   - Description: This task is created to track the progress and implementation of the newly requested feature. Please include all relevant details, requirements, and acceptance criteria for the feature. Assign appropriate team members and set due dates as necessary.\n   - Priority: Set according to the urgency of the feature request (e.g., Medium or High).\n   - Assignee: Assign to the relevant team member or product owner.\n   - Labels/Tags: Add labels such as \"feature request\", \"new feature\", or any project-specific tags.\n   - Attachments: If there are any supporting documents or specifications, attach them to the task.\n5. Review the information for accuracy and completeness.\n6. Click \"Create\", \"Add Task\", or the equivalent button to save the new task in the main project workspace.\n7. Notify relevant stakeholders (via comments, mentions, or notifications) that the new feature request task has been created and is ready for tracking.\n\nThis process ensures the new feature request is properly documented, tracked, and visible to all relevant team members in the main project workspace.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:440:      "response": "To create a task in Asana based on a new HubSpot deal and ensure synchronized workflows for the sales and project teams, follow these comprehensive steps:\n\n1. Integrate HubSpot and Asana:\n   - Use a native integration (if available) or a third-party automation tool like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat).\n   - In Zapier, create a new Zap with the following setup:\n     - Trigger: \"New Deal in HubSpot\" (choose the appropriate pipeline/stage if needed).\n     - Action: \"Create Task in Asana\".\n\n2. Configure the Trigger (HubSpot):\n   - Connect your HubSpot account to Zapier.\n   - Set the trigger event to \"New Deal\".\n   - Optionally, filter by pipeline or deal stage to only trigger for relevant deals.\n\n3. Set Up the Action (Asana):\n   - Connect your Asana account to Zapier.\n   - Choose the workspace and project where the task should be created.\n   - Map HubSpot deal fields to Asana task fields:\n     - Task Name: Use the deal name (e.g., \"New Deal: {{Deal Name}}\").\n     - Description: Include relevant deal details such as deal amount, close date, deal owner, company name, and any custom fields.\n     - Assignee: Assign to the relevant project manager or team member.\n     - Due Date: Set based on the deal close date or a standard turnaround time.\n     - Attachments/Links: Add a link to the HubSpot deal record for easy reference.\n\n4. Test the Integration:\n   - Create a test deal in HubSpot to ensure a corresponding task is created in Asana with all mapped details.\n\n5. Notify Teams:\n   - Optionally, add an additional action in Zapier to send a notification (e.g., Slack message or email) to alert the project team of the new task.\n\n6. Maintain and Optimize:\n   - Periodically review the integration to ensure it meets evolving workflow needs.\n   - Update field mappings or add custom logic as required.\n\nExample Asana Task Created:\n- Task Name: \"New Deal: Acme Corp Website Redesign\"\n- Description:\n  ```\n  A new deal has been closed in HubSpot.\n\n  Deal Details:\n  - Deal Name: Acme Corp Website Redesign\n  - Amount: $25,000\n  - Close Date: 2024-07-01\n  - Deal Owner: Jane Smith\n  - Company: Acme Corp\n  - HubSpot Deal Link: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/123456\n\n  Please review and initiate the project onboarding process.\n  ```\n- Assignee: John Doe (Project Manager)\n- Due Date: 2024-07-03\n\nBy following these steps, you ensure that every new HubSpot deal automatically creates a detailed, actionable task in Asana, keeping sales and project teams aligned and workflows synchronized.",

Configure the Trigger (HubSpot):\n   - Connect your HubSpot account to Zapier.\n   - Set the trigger event to \"New Deal\".\n   - Optionally, filter by pipeline or deal stage to only trigger for relevant deals.\n\n3. Set Up the Action (Asana):\n   - Connect your Asana account to Zapier.\n   - Choose the workspace and project where the task should be created.\n   - Map HubSpot deal fields to Asana task fields:\n     - Task Name: Use the deal name (e.g., \"New Deal: {{Deal Name}}\").\n     - Description: Include relevant deal details such as deal amount, close date, deal owner, company name, and any custom fields.\n     - Assignee: Assign to the relevant project manager or team member.\n     - Due Date: Set based on the deal close date or a standard turnaround time.\n     - Attachments/Links: Add a link to the HubSpot deal record for easy reference.\n\n4. Set Up the Action (Asana):\n   - Connect your Asana account to Zapier.\n   - Choose the workspace and project where the task should be created.\n   - Map HubSpot deal fields to Asana task fields:\n     - Task Name: Use the deal name (e.g., \"New Deal: {{Deal Name}}\").\n     - Description: Include relevant deal details such as deal amount, close date, deal owner, company name, and any custom fields.\n     - Assignee: Assign to the relevant project manager or team member.\n     - Due Date: Set based on the deal close date or a standard turnaround time.\n     - Attachments/Links: Add a link to the HubSpot deal record for easy reference.\n\n4. Integrate HubSpot and Asana:\n   - Use a native integration (if available) or a third-party automation tool like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat).\n   - In Zapier, create a new Zap with the following setup:\n     - Trigger: \"New Deal in HubSpot\" (choose the appropriate pipeline/stage if needed).\n     - Action: \"Create Task in Asana\".\n\n2. Configure the Trigger (HubSpot):\n   - Connect your HubSpot account to Zapier.\n   - Set the trigger event to \"New Deal\".\n   - Optionally, filter by pipeline or deal stage to only trigger for relevant deals.\n\n3. Set Up the Action (Asana):\n   - Connect your Asana account to Zapier.\n   - Choose the workspace and project where the task should be created.\n   - Map HubSpot deal fields to Asana task fields:\n     - Task Name: Use the deal name (e.g., \"New Deal: {{Deal Name}}\").\n     - Description: Include relevant deal details such as deal amount, close date, deal owner, company name, and any custom fields.\n     - Assignee: Assign to the relevant project manager or team member.\n     - Due Date: Set based on the deal close date or a standard turnaround time.\n     - Attachments/Links: Add a link to the HubSpot deal record for easy reference.\n\n4. Test the Integration:\n   - Create a test deal in HubSpot to ensure a corresponding task is created in Asana with all mapped details.\n\n5. Notify Teams:\n   - Optionally, add an additional action in Zapier to send a notification (e.g., Slack message or email) to alert the project team of the new task.\n\n6. Maintain and Optimize:\n   - Periodically review the integration to ensure it meets evolving workflow needs.\n   - Update field mappings or add custom logic as required.\n\nExample Asana Task Created:\n- Task Name: \"New Deal: Acme Corp Website Redesign\"\n- Description:\n  ```\n  A new deal has been closed in HubSpot.\n\n  Deal Details:\n  - Deal Name: Acme Corp Website Redesign\n  - Amount: $25,000\n  - Close Date: 2024-07-01\n  - Deal Owner: Jane Smith\n  - Company: Acme Corp\n  - HubSpot Deal Link: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/123456\n\n  Please review and initiate the project onboarding process.\n  ```\n- Assignee: John Doe (Project Manager)\n- Due Date: 2024-07-03\n\nBy following these steps, you ensure that every new HubSpot deal automatically creates a detailed, actionable task in Asana, keeping sales and project teams aligned and workflows synchronized.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:639:      "response": "To provide a detailed status of a specific Asana project, including tasks and progress, follow these steps:\n\n1. Access the Asana Project:\n   - Log in to your Asana account.\n   - Navigate to the specific project you want to review from the sidebar or search bar.\n\n2. Project Overview:\n   - At the top of the project, you\u2019ll see the project name, owner, and due date (if set).\n   - The \u201cOverview\u201d tab provides a summary, including project description, key milestones, and recent activity.\n\n3. Task List and Progress:\n   - Click on the \u201cList\u201d or \u201cBoard\u201d view to see all tasks.\n   - Each task displays its name, assignee, due date, and completion status.\n   - Sections or columns may represent phases or categories.\n   - Completed tasks are usually checked off or moved to a \u201cDone\u201d section.\n\n4. Progress Tab:\n   - The \u201cProgress\u201d tab (if enabled) shows a visual progress chart (e.g., % complete, number of tasks done vs. total).\n   - You can see recent status updates, blockers, and next steps.\n   - Project owners can post regular status updates with color-coded health indicators (On Track, At Risk, Off Track).\n\n5. Task Details:\n   - Click on any task to view details: description, subtasks, attachments, comments, and custom fields (e.g., priority, effort).\n   - Subtasks have their own completion status.\n\n6. Reporting and Metrics:\n   - Use the \u201cDashboard\u201d or \u201cReporting\u201d features to generate charts (e.g., tasks by status, overdue tasks, workload by assignee).\n   - Export data to CSV for further analysis if needed.\n\n7. Example of a Detailed Project Status Report:\nProject: Website Redesign\nOwner: Jane Doe\nDue Date: 2024-07-15\n\nProject Health: On Track (Green)\nLast Update: 2024-06-10\n\nSummary:\n- Total Tasks: 42\n- Completed: 28\n- In Progress: 10\n- Not Started: 4\n- Overdue: 2\n\nMilestones:\n- Wireframes Approved (Completed)\n- Content Drafted (In Progress)\n- Development Started (In Progress)\n- Launch (Not Started)\n\nKey Tasks:\n1. Design Homepage (Completed)\n2. Write About Us Content (In Progress, Due 2024-06-12, Assigned to John Smith)\n3. Develop Contact Form (Not Started, Due 2024-06-20, Assigned to Sarah Lee)\n4. QA Testing (Not Started, Due 2024-07-05, Assigned to QA Team)\n\nBlockers:\n- Awaiting final logo from branding team (affects homepage deployment)\n- Two overdue tasks: \u201cFinalize Footer Design\u201d and \u201cMobile Responsiveness Testing\u201d\n\nNext Steps:\n- Complete content writing by 2024-06-12\n- Begin QA testing by 2024-07-01\n\nCharts:\n- Progress: 67% tasks completed\n- Overdue Tasks: 2 (5% of total)\n- Workload: Evenly distributed among team members\n\nAttachments:\n- Latest wireframes (attached to \u201cDesign Homepage\u201d task)\n- Content drafts (attached to respective tasks)\n\nRecent Comments:\n- \u201cAwaiting feedback from client on About Us page.\u201d \u2013 John Smith, 2024-06-09\n\nFor a live, up-to-date report, use Asana\u2019s \u201cDashboard\u201d or export the project data for executive review.\n\nThis comprehensive status includes all key metrics and details required for an executive review, directly reflecting the current state of the Asana project.",

This visual overview helps identify who is overloaded or underutilized.\n\n2. Gather Task Details:\n   - Ensure all tasks have clear due dates, priorities, and estimated effort assigned.\n   - If effort estimates are missing, collaborate with task owners to add them for accurate workload calculation.\n\n3. Analyze Resource Allocation:\n   - In the Workload view, look for team members whose workload bars are red (over capacity) or green (under capacity).\n   - Click on individual team members to see detailed breakdowns of their assigned tasks.\n\n4. Reassign Tasks for Balance:\n   - For overloaded team members, identify tasks that can be reassigned.\n   - Drag and drop tasks from overloaded members to those with available capacity directly in the Workload view.\n   - Alternatively, open the task, click \u201cAssignee,\u201d and select a new team member.\n\n5. Communicate Changes:\n   - Notify team members of any changes in their assignments via Asana comments or team messages.\n   - Provide context for reassignments to ensure clarity and maintain morale.\n\n6. Automate and Monitor:\n   - Set up Asana rules to automatically assign new tasks based on workload or role.\n   - Regularly review the Workload tab to monitor ongoing balance and make adjustments as needed.\n\n7. opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:935:      "response": "To set up Slack reminders for upcoming Asana task deadlines and ensure your team receives proactive deadline notifications, follow these comprehensive steps using the official Asana and Slack integration:\n\n1. **Connect Asana to Slack:**\n   - In Slack, go to the Apps section and search for \u201cAsana.\u201d\n   - Click \u201cAdd to Slack\u201d and follow the prompts to connect your Asana account.\n   - Authorize the integration by logging into your Asana account when prompted.\n\n2. **Choose the Slack Channel for Notifications:**\n   - Decide which Slack channel(s) should receive Asana task deadline reminders (e.g., #project-deadlines).\n   - In Slack, type `/asana link` and select the relevant Asana project(s) you want to monitor.\n\n3. **Configure Asana Notifications in Slack:**\n   - In the linked channel, type `/asana` to see available commands.\n   - Use `/asana settings` to customize which notifications are sent to the channel.\n   - Enable notifications for \u201cTask due soon\u201d and \u201cTask due date changed\u201d to ensure reminders are sent as deadlines approach.\n\n4. **Set Up Personal Reminders (Optional):**\n   - Team members can type `/asana my tasks` in Slack to see their upcoming tasks.\n   - They can also set up personal reminders in Asana by clicking the bell icon on a task and choosing \u201cRemind me\u201d (e.g., 1 day before due).\n\n5. **Automate Custom Reminders with Asana Rules (Advanced):**\n   - In Asana, open your project and click \u201cCustomize\u201d in the top right.\n   - Under \u201cRules,\u201d click \u201cAdd Rule.\u201d\n   - Choose a trigger like \u201cDue date is approaching\u201d (e.g., 1 day before due).\n   - Set the action to \u201cSend a message to Slack\u201d and select the appropriate channel.\n   - Save the rule. Now, whenever a task\u2019s due date is approaching, a reminder will be automatically posted in Slack.\n\n6. **Test the Integration:**\n   - Create a test task in Asana with a due date within the next day.\n   - Confirm that a reminder appears in the designated Slack channel at the expected time.\n\n7. **Best Practices:**\n   - Regularly review which projects are linked to which Slack channels to avoid notification overload.\n   - Encourage team members to use Asana\u2019s \u201cMy Tasks\u201d and Slack\u2019s personal reminders for individual accountability.\n   - Adjust notification settings as needed to match your team\u2019s workflow.\n\nBy following these steps, your team will receive timely Slack reminders for upcoming Asana task deadlines, ensuring everyone stays on track and proactive about meeting project goals.",

Here is a comprehensive step-by-step solution using Zapier, which is widely used and user-friendly:\n\n**Step 1: Set Up Your Tools**\n- Ensure you have admin access to HubSpot, Asana, and Slack.\n- Create accounts on Zapier (https://zapier.com/) if you don\u2019t have one.\n\n**Step 2: Create a New Zap in Zapier**\n1. **Trigger: HubSpot New Contact**\n   - App: HubSpot\n   - Trigger Event: New Contact\n   - Connect your HubSpot account.\n   - Set up the trigger to fire when a new contact is created (you can filter by specific properties if needed).\n\n2. **Action: Create Task in Asana**\n   - App: Asana\n   - Action Event: Create Task\n   - Connect your Asana account.\n   - Set up the action:\n     - Choose the Workspace and Project where tasks should be created.\n     - Map HubSpot contact fields (e.g., Name, Email, Company, Phone) into the Asana task fields (e.g., Task Name, Description, Custom Fields).\n     - Optionally assign the task to a specific team member or set due dates based on your process.\n\n3. **Action: Send Notification in Slack**\n   - App: Slack\n   - Action Event: Send Channel Message (or Direct Message)\n   - Connect your Slack workspace.\n   - Set up the message:\n     - Choose the channel (e.g., #operations-leads) or user to notify.\n     - Customize the message to include key contact details from HubSpot and a link to the new Asana task (Zapier can return the Asana task URL).\n     - Example message:  \n       ```\n       :bell: New Lead Synced!\n       Name: {{HubSpot Contact Name}}\n       Email: {{HubSpot Contact Email}}\n       Asana Task: {{Asana Task URL}}\n       ```\n   - Optionally, use Slack\u2019s formatting for better readability.\n\n**Step 3: Test the Workflow**\n- Use Zapier\u2019s built-in test features to ensure that when a new contact is added in HubSpot, a corresponding task is created in Asana and a Slack notification is sent.\n- Adjust field mappings and message formatting as needed.\n\n**Step 4: Turn On the Zap**\n- Once testing is successful, turn on the Zap to make the workflow live.\n\n**Step 5: Monitor and Maintain**\n- Periodically check Zapier\u2019s task history for errors.\n- Update field mappings if your HubSpot or Asana structures change.\n\n**Optional Enhancements:**\n- Add filters in Zapier to only sync contacts that meet certain criteria (e.g., lead status, lifecycle stage).\n- Use Asana custom fields to track lead status or priority.\n- Add additional Slack actions, such as mentioning specific users or using Slack threads for follow-up.\n\n**Summary of Workflow:**\n1. New contact is created in HubSpot.\n2. Zapier automatically creates a new task in Asana with the contact\u2019s details.\n3. Zapier sends a notification to a designated Slack channel or user with the lead info and Asana task link.\n4. Operations team is instantly notified and can take action, ensuring a seamless lead-to-task pipeline.\n\n**Tools Used:**\n- HubSpot (CRM)\n- Asana (Task Management)\n- Slack (Team Communication)\n- Zapier (Automation Platform)\n\nThis setup ensures a fully automated, reliable, and scalable lead-to-task pipeline for your operations team, with real-time notifications and no manual intervention required.",

Here is a comprehensive step-by-step solution using Zapier, which is widely used and user-friendly:\n\n**Step 1: Set Up Your Tools**\n- Ensure you have admin access to HubSpot, Asana, and Slack.\n- Create accounts on Zapier (https://zapier.com/) if you don\u2019t have one.\n\n**Step 2: Create a New Zap in Zapier**\n1. **Trigger: HubSpot New Contact**\n   - App: HubSpot\n   - Trigger Event: New Contact\n   - Connect your HubSpot account.\n   - Set up the trigger to fire when a new contact is created (you can filter by specific properties if needed).\n\n2. **Action: Create Task in Asana**\n   - App: Asana\n   - Action Event: Create Task\n   - Connect your Asana account.\n   - Set up the action:\n     - Choose the Workspace and Project where tasks should be created.\n     - Map HubSpot contact fields (e.g., Name, Email, Company, Phone) into the Asana task fields (e.g., Task Name, Description, Custom Fields).\n     - Optionally assign the task to a specific team member or set due dates based on your process.\n\n3. **Action: Send Notification in Slack**\n   - App: Slack\n   - Action Event: Send Channel Message (or Direct Message)\n   - Connect your Slack workspace.\n   - Set up the message:\n     - Choose the channel (e.g., #operations-leads) or user to notify.\n     - Customize the message to include key contact details from HubSpot and a link to the new Asana task (Zapier can return the Asana task URL).\n     - Example message:  \n       ```\n       :bell: New Lead Synced!\n       Name: {{HubSpot Contact Name}}\n       Email: {{HubSpot Contact Email}}\n       Asana Task: {{Asana Task URL}}\n       ```\n   - Optionally, use Slack\u2019s formatting for better readability.\n\n**Step 3: Test the Workflow**\n- Use Zapier\u2019s built-in test features to ensure that when a new contact is added in HubSpot, a corresponding task is created in Asana and a Slack notification is sent.\n- Adjust field mappings and message formatting as needed.\n\n**Step 4: Turn On the Zap**\n- Once testing is successful, turn on the Zap to make the workflow live.\n\n**Step 5: Monitor and Maintain**\n- Periodically check Zapier\u2019s task history for errors.\n- Update field mappings if your HubSpot or Asana structures change.\n\n**Optional Enhancements:**\n- Add filters in Zapier to only sync contacts that meet certain criteria (e.g., lead status, lifecycle stage).\n- Use Asana custom fields to track lead status or priority.\n- Add additional Slack actions, such as mentioning specific users or using Slack threads for follow-up.\n\n**Summary of Workflow:**\n1. New contact is created in HubSpot.\n2. Zapier automatically creates a new task in Asana with the contact\u2019s details.\n3. Zapier sends a notification to a designated Slack channel or user with the lead info and Asana task link.\n4. Operations team is instantly notified and can take action, ensuring a seamless lead-to-task pipeline.\n\n**Tools Used:**\n- HubSpot (CRM)\n- Asana (Task Management)\n- Slack (Team Communication)\n- Zapier (Automation Platform)\n\nThis setup ensures a fully automated, reliable, and scalable lead-to-task pipeline for your operations team, with real-time notifications and no manual intervention required.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:1172:      "agent_response_raw": "To list all active projects in your Asana workspace, follow these steps:\n\n1. Log in to your Asana account at https://app.asana.com/.\n2. In the left sidebar, click on the name of your workspace or organization to ensure you are in the correct workspace.\n3. Click on the \u201cProjects\u201d tab or section in the sidebar. This will display a list of all projects in the workspace.\n4. To filter for active projects:\n   - If you use Asana\u2019s default views, active projects are typically those not archived. Archived projects are hidden by default.\n   - If you see archived projects, you can filter them out by clicking the filter icon (three horizontal lines or funnel icon) and selecting \u201cActive\u201d or deselecting \u201cArchived.\u201d\n5. Review the list of active projects displayed. Each project will show its name, and you can click into each for more details.\n\nAlternatively, if you have access to Asana\u2019s advanced search:\n1. Click the search bar at the top and select \u201cAdvanced Search.\u201d\n2. Set the filter to \u201cProjects\u201d and add criteria such as \u201cNot Archived.\u201d\n3. Run the search to display all active projects.\n\nIf you are using the Asana API or integrations:\n- Use the endpoint GET /projects with the parameter archived=false to retrieve all active projects programmatically.\n\nHere is an example of what the list might look like (replace with your actual project names):\n\n- Marketing Campaign Q2\n- Product Launch Roadmap\n- Website Redesign\n- Customer Support Improvements\n- Weekly Standup Planning\n- Sales Pipeline Management\n\nThis list will give you a comprehensive overview of all active projects in your Asana workspace for your weekly standup review. Locate the option to create a new task, issue, or card (usually a \"+ New Task\", \"Create Issue\", or similar button).\n3. Click the button to open the new task creation form.\n4. Fill in the task details as follows:\n   - Title: Track New Feature Request\n   - Description: This task is created to track the progress and implementation of the newly requested feature. Please include all relevant details, requirements, and acceptance criteria for the feature. Assign appropriate team members and set due dates as necessary.\n   - Priority: Set according to the urgency of the feature request (e.g., Medium or High).\n   - Assignee: Assign to the relevant team member or product owner.\n   - Labels/Tags: Add labels such as \"feature request\", \"new feature\", or any project-specific tags.\n   - Attachments: If there are any supporting documents or specifications, attach them to the task.\n5. Review the information for accuracy and completeness.\n6. Click \"Create\", \"Add Task\", or the equivalent button to save the new task in the main project workspace.\n7. Notify relevant stakeholders (via comments, mentions, or notifications) that the new feature request task has been created and is ready for tracking.\n\nThis process ensures the new feature request is properly documented, tracked, and visible to all relevant team members in the main project workspace.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:1196:      "agent_response_raw": "To create a task in Asana based on a new HubSpot deal and ensure synchronized workflows for the sales and project teams, follow these comprehensive steps:\n\n1. Integrate HubSpot and Asana:\n   - Use a native integration (if available) or a third-party automation tool like Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat).\n   - In Zapier, create a new Zap with the following setup:\n     - Trigger: \"New Deal in HubSpot\" (choose the appropriate pipeline/stage if needed).\n     - Action: \"Create Task in Asana\".\n\n2. Configure the Trigger (HubSpot):\n   - Connect your HubSpot account to Zapier.\n   - Set the trigger event to \"New Deal\".\n   - Optionally, filter by pipeline or deal stage to only trigger for relevant deals.\n\n3. Set Up the Action (Asana):\n   - Connect your Asana account to Zapier.\n   - Choose the workspace and project where the task should be created.\n   - Map HubSpot deal fields to Asana task fields:\n     - Task Name: Use the deal name (e.g., \"New Deal: {{Deal Name}}\").\n     - Description: Include relevant deal details such as deal amount, close date, deal owner, company name, and any custom fields.\n     - Assignee: Assign to the relevant project manager or team member.\n     - Due Date: Set based on the deal close date or a standard turnaround time.\n     - Attachments/Links: Add a link to the HubSpot deal record for easy reference.\n\n4. Test the Integration:\n   - Create a test deal in HubSpot to ensure a corresponding task is created in Asana with all mapped details.\n\n5. Notify Teams:\n   - Optionally, add an additional action in Zapier to send a notification (e.g., Slack message or email) to alert the project team of the new task.\n\n6. Maintain and Optimize:\n   - Periodically review the integration to ensure it meets evolving workflow needs.\n   - Update field mappings or add custom logic as required.\n\nExample Asana Task Created:\n- Task Name: \"New Deal: Acme Corp Website Redesign\"\n- Description:\n  ```\n  A new deal has been closed in HubSpot.\n\n  Deal Details:\n  - Deal Name: Acme Corp Website Redesign\n  - Amount: $25,000\n  - Close Date: 2024-07-01\n  - Deal Owner: Jane Smith\n  - Company: Acme Corp\n  - HubSpot Deal Link: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/123456\n\n  Please review and initiate the project onboarding process.\n  ```\n- Assignee: John Doe (Project Manager)\n- Due Date: 2024-07-03\n\nBy following these steps, you ensure that every new HubSpot deal automatically creates a detailed, actionable task in Asana, keeping sales and project teams aligned and workflows synchronized.",

opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:1220:      "agent_response_raw": "To provide a detailed status of a specific Asana project, including tasks and progress, follow these steps:\n\n1. Access the Asana Project:\n   - Log in to your Asana account.\n   - Navigate to the specific project you want to review from the sidebar or search bar.\n\n2. Project Overview:\n   - At the top of the project, you\u2019ll see the project name, owner, and due date (if set).\n   - The \u201cOverview\u201d tab provides a summary, including project description, key milestones, and recent activity.\n\n3. Task List and Progress:\n   - Click on the \u201cList\u201d or \u201cBoard\u201d view to see all tasks.\n   - Each task displays its name, assignee, due date, and completion status.\n   - Sections or columns may represent phases or categories.\n   - Completed tasks are usually checked off or moved to a \u201cDone\u201d section.\n\n4. Progress Tab:\n   - The \u201cProgress\u201d tab (if enabled) shows a visual progress chart (e.g., % complete, number of tasks done vs. total).\n   - You can see recent status updates, blockers, and next steps.\n   - Project owners can post regular status updates with color-coded health indicators (On Track, At Risk, Off Track).\n\n5. Task Details:\n   - Click on any task to view details: description, subtasks, attachments, comments, and custom fields (e.g., priority, effort).\n   - Subtasks have their own completion status.\n\n6. Reporting and Metrics:\n   - Use the \u201cDashboard\u201d or \u201cReporting\u201d features to generate charts (e.g., tasks by status, overdue tasks, workload by assignee).\n   - Export data to CSV for further analysis if needed.\n\n7. Example of a Detailed Project Status Report:\nProject: Website Redesign\nOwner: Jane Doe\nDue Date: 2024-07-15\n\nProject Health: On Track (Green)\nLast Update: 2024-06-10\n\nSummary:\n- Total Tasks: 42\n- Completed: 28\n- In Progress: 10\n- Not Started: 4\n- Overdue: 2\n\nMilestones:\n- Wireframes Approved (Completed)\n- Content Drafted (In Progress)\n- Development Started (In Progress)\n- Launch (Not Started)\n\nKey Tasks:\n1. Design Homepage (Completed)\n2. Write About Us Content (In Progress, Due 2024-06-12, Assigned to John Smith)\n3. Develop Contact Form (Not Started, Due 2024-06-20, Assigned to Sarah Lee)\n4. Project Overview:\n   - At the top of the project, you\u2019ll see the project name, owner, and due date (if set).\n   - The \u201cOverview\u201d tab provides a summary, including project description, key milestones, and recent activity.\n\n3. Task List and Progress:\n   - Click on the \u201cList\u201d or \u201cBoard\u201d view to see all tasks.\n   - Each task displays its name, assignee, due date, and completion status.\n   - Sections or columns may represent phases or categories.\n   - Completed tasks are usually checked off or moved to a \u201cDone\u201d section.\n\n4. Progress Tab:\n   - The \u201cProgress\u201d tab (if enabled) shows a visual progress chart (e.g., % complete, number of tasks done vs. total).\n   - You can see recent status updates, blockers, and next steps.\n   - Project owners can post regular status updates with color-coded health indicators (On Track, At Risk, Off Track).\n\n5. Task Details:\n   - Click on any task to view details: description, subtasks, attachments, comments, and custom fields (e.g., priority, effort).\n   - Subtasks have their own completion status.\n\n6. Reporting and Metrics:\n   - Use the \u201cDashboard\u201d or \u201cReporting\u201d features to generate charts (e.g., tasks by status, overdue tasks, workload by assignee).\n   - Export data to CSV for further analysis if needed.\n\n7. opspal/enhanced_exam_outputs/strict_exam_20250805_101450.json:1244:      "agent_response_raw": "To assign tasks to team members based on workload in Asana and optimize resource allocation, follow these comprehensive steps:\n\n1. Review Current Workload:\n   - Open Asana and navigate to the \u201cWorkload\u201d tab under the \u201cPortfolios\u201d feature (available in Asana Business and Enterprise plans).\n   - Review each team member\u2019s current tasks, deadlines, and estimated effort (hours or points). This visual overview helps identify who is overloaded or underutilized.\n\n2. Gather Task Details:\n   - Ensure all tasks have clear due dates, priorities, and estimated effort assigned.\n   - If effort estimates are missing, collaborate with task owners to add them for accurate workload calculation.\n\n3. Analyze Resource Allocation:\n   - In the Workload view, look for team members whose workload bars are red (over capacity) or green (under capacity).\n   - Click on individual team members to see detailed breakdowns of their assigned tasks.\n\n4. Reassign Tasks for Balance:\n   - For overloaded team members, identify tasks that can be reassigned.\n   - Drag and drop tasks from overloaded members to those with available capacity directly in the Workload view.\n   - Alternatively, open the task, click \u201cAssignee,\u201d and select a new team member.\n\n5. Communicate Changes:\n   - Notify team members of any changes in their assignments via Asana comments or team messages.\n   - Provide context for reassignments to ensure clarity and maintain morale.\n\n6. Automate and Monitor:\n   - Set up Asana rules to automatically assign new tasks based on workload or role.\n   - Regularly review the Workload tab to monitor ongoing balance and make adjustments as needed.\n\n7. Continuous Improvement:\n   - Hold regular resource allocation meetings to discuss workload and gather feedback.\n   - Adjust estimation practices and task distribution based on team input and project outcomes.\n\nBy following these steps and leveraging Asana\u2019s Workload and automation features, you can ensure tasks are assigned efficiently, team members are neither overburdened nor underutilized, and resource allocation is continuously optimized.",

Review Current Workload:\n   - Open Asana and navigate to the \u201cWorkload\u201d tab under the \u201cPortfolios\u201d feature (available in Asana Business and Enterprise plans).\n   - Review each team member\u2019s current tasks, deadlines, and estimated effort (hours or points). This visual overview helps identify who is overloaded or underutilized.\n\n2. Gather Task Details:\n   - Ensure all tasks have clear due dates, priorities, and estimated effort assigned.\n   - If effort estimates are missing, collaborate with task owners to add them for accurate workload calculation.\n\n3. Analyze Resource Allocation:\n   - In the Workload view, look for team members whose workload bars are red (over capacity) or green (under capacity).\n   - Click on individual team members to see detailed breakdowns of their assigned tasks.\n\n4. Reassign Tasks for Balance:\n   - For overloaded team members, identify tasks that can be reassigned.\n   - Drag and drop tasks from overloaded members to those with available capacity directly in the Workload view.\n   - Alternatively, open the task, click \u201cAssignee,\u201d and select a new team member.\n\n5. Communicate Changes:\n   - Notify team members of any changes in their assignments via Asana comments or team messages.\n   - Provide context for reassignments to ensure clarity and maintain morale.\n\n6. Automate and Monitor:\n   - Set up Asana rules to automatically assign new tasks based on workload or role.\n   - Regularly review the Workload tab to monitor ongoing balance and make adjustments as needed.\n\n7.
